name: Rectangular Microstrip Patch
description: A rectangular conducting patch printed on a grounded dielectric substrate, operating as a resonant cavity antenna. The patch radiates primarily from the fringing fields at the edges perpendicular to the current flow. It is one of the most widely used microstrip antenna configurations due to its simple geometry and predictable performance.

patch_length.name: Patch Length
patch_length.description: Length of the rectangular patch in the direction of current flow

patch_width.name: Patch Width
patch_width.description: Width of the rectangular patch perpendicular to current flow

dielectric_constant.name: Relative Permittivity
dielectric_constant.description: Relative dielectric constant of the substrate material

loss_tangent.name: Loss Tangent
loss_tangent.description: Dielectric loss tangent of the substrate material

feed_position_x.name: Feed Position X
feed_position_x.description: X-coordinate of feed point from patch center

feed_position_y.name: Feed Position Y
feed_position_y.description: Y-coordinate of feed point from patch center

designMethodology.overview: The design process involves calculating the patch dimensions for resonance at the desired frequency, accounting for fringing field effects and substrate properties.

steps.title: Calculate Patch Width
steps.description: Determine the optimal width for maximum radiation efficiency

steps.title: Calculate Effective Dielectric Constant
steps.description: Account for fringing fields in the effective permittivity

steps.title: Calculate Extension Length
steps.description: Determine the equivalent extension due to fringing fields

steps.title: Calculate Patch Length
steps.description: Determine the resonant length accounting for extensions

steps.title: Calculate Input Resistance
steps.description: Determine the input resistance at the edge feed
steps.formula: Rin = 1/(2*G1) where G1 = I1/(120*π^2) * integral of radiation conductance

steps.title: Optimize Feed Position
steps.description: Adjust feed location for desired input impedance

equations.inputImpedance: Zin = Rin + j*Xin where Rin = cos^2(π*yf/L)/(2*G1)

references: Balanis, C.A., 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Wiley, 2016
Stutzman, W.L. and Thiele, G.A., 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Wiley, 2012
Volakis, J.L., 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, McGraw-Hill, 2007
Garg, R., 'Microstrip Antenna Design Handbook', Artech House, 2001
